title: Neighbors question safety at Syracuse city pool after fatal shooting
summary: A fatal shooting of a 15-year-old outside Onondaga Park Pool on June 25 has sparked community safety concerns and criticism of the city's response. Mayor Walsh promised increased police presence at pools, but residents report seeing no visible officer patrols at three visited facilities (Kirk Park, Lincoln Park, McKinley Park) on June 26, raising questions about implementation and transparency.
department_refs: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs

political_signal.description: Community members expressing public doubt about municipal commitment to public safety promises; residents report gap between mayoral announcement and ground-level implementation, generating reputational risk around government transparency and service delivery.

processing_notes: Incident date discrepancy: article dated June 26 but references shooting on June 25 (Tuesday); lead states 'a day after' shooting
No specific budget or procurement data mentioned
Investigation ongoing; Onondaga Park Pool remains closed
Potential IT/data governance angle: real-time police dispatch visibility and community communication systems
Related escalating trend: article references multiple shooting incidents within 24 hours in Syracuse
